driver: playwright/chromium against dev server :3100 (DEV_AUTH + LOCAL_DB); 2 attempts (warm retry)
scope: Eva × brief-to-publishable-draft on public /ai-asistent (Obsahový brief). Real Claude-CLI model calls.

findings.title: The brief→article-draft loop doesn't close in dev — generations race a 60s client ceiling; the draft step times out, the brief is flaky at the boundary
findings.expected: Eva goes topic → brief → publishable draft in one continuous loop.
findings.got: Brief: attempt 1 timed out at 60s; attempt 2 completed at ~48s (flaky boundary). Article-draft ('Rozepsat článek'): timed out at 60s — she never reaches the publishable draft. UI shows 'Model neodpověděl do 60 sekund. Zkusit znovu.'
findings.code_check: confirmed — useAiTool.ts:14 AI_TIMEOUT_MS=60_000 hard-aborts via AbortController at 60s; server returned 200 at exactly 60s (right at the ceiling). INCONSISTENCY: the /app campaigns analyze route has no equivalent client abort (Tomáš's eval ran 104s to success), so the same class of slow model call is killed on /ai-asistent but tolerated on /app.
findings.scope_note: Root cause is the DEV provider latency (Claude CLI cold spawn, ~47–104s). Prod uses gemini-3-flash-preview, which is likely faster and may not hit this — but on this dev deployment a real user cannot complete the loop, and the per-surface inconsistency is a code issue regardless of provider.
findings.suggested_acceptance: Align the client ceiling to realistic provider latency (raise it and/or stream partial output) instead of a hard 60s abort, and make the timeout policy consistent across surfaces (assistant vs. campaigns analyze). Heavier generations (article-draft) need more headroom than the brief.
findings.resolution: FIXED (see EV1-fix.md). useAiTool.AI_TIMEOUT_MS is now env-aware (prod 60s; dev = CLAUDE_TIMEOUT_MS+30s) and CLAUDE_TIMEOUT_MS raised 90s->150s for the heavy draft. Live re-drive: brief done ~50-74s, article-draft done ~126s — loop closes. Prod ceiling unchanged.

findings.title: The generated brief fails two of its own scorecard checks (keyword missing in meta + intro)
findings.expected: A brief that satisfies the very SEO rules the tool then grades it against.
findings.got: Scorecard flags 'Klíčové slovo chybí v meta popisku' and 'chybí v první sekci osnovy' — the generator produced a brief its own checker marks down. (The transparency is a plus; the self-inconsistency is the gap.)
findings.suggested_acceptance: Feed the scorecard rules into the generation prompt (or a self-check pass) so the brief satisfies its own keyword-coverage checks before it's shown.
findings.resolution: FIXED. brief.ts now explicitly requires the primary keyword in the title, meta description AND the first H2 heading (the exact three scoreBrief checks). Prompt-only — no added latency. Live re-drive: all three keyword-coverage chips now report 'je v...' (was 'chybí v meta popisku' + 'chybí v první sekci osnovy').

passed: When the brief loads, quality is high and BRAND-SPECIFIC (Mionelo = nuts/seeds e-shop): outline covers oxidation/žluknutí, containers, temperature, shelf-life by type, freezing, mistakes, spoilage — not generic boilerplate. Clears Eva's #1 quality bar.
SERP preview is present and realistic (desktop/mobile toggle, real slug/title/meta) — she can judge before committing.
Scorecard is honest: SEO score 75/100 with readability, keyword-coverage and E-E-A-T checks, including gaps (rare for an AI tool to mark down its own output).
Title/meta respect SEO limits (title 46/60, meta 129/155); 7-section outline, 4 FAQ, 8 keywords, 4 on-brand internal-link suggestions.
Transparency + export: shows model (claude-sonnet) + latency + $0 cost, a 'show the prompt' panel, and .md export — so even a brief-only outcome is usable.
